recommendations.thesis: Israel's strikes on Iran's nuclear program and IRGC leadership killed the top military commander, dramatically escalating conflict. LMT as the primary F-35 supplier to Israel is directly in the demand path for defense spending acceleration.
recommendations.catalyst: Israel struck Iran on June 13 killing IRGC commander-in-chief; defense spending expectations surging

catalystClarity.reasoning: Concrete military escalation with named casualties and Israeli nuclear-site targeting creates clear near-term defense spending tailwind

technicalSetup.reasoning: LMT gapped up 3.66% Friday to $491.95 — entering after confirmation of geopolitical catalyst, though now extended

riskReward.reasoning: Target $510 (+3.7%), stop $478 (-2.8%), R/R ~1.3:1 — modest but acceptable given catalyst clarity

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: LMT is highly liquid large-cap defense stock, tight spreads expected Monday morning

marketAlignment.reasoning: Broader market sold off 1.1% Friday; defense sector bucked the trend but overall risk-off environment is a headwind

informationEdge.reasoning: Clear pre-market news: Israel killed Iran's most senior military official, attacks targeting nuclear program — widely reported Friday

timingUrgency.reasoning: Iran has not yet counter-escalated fully; weekend developments may intensify the conflict, making Monday open a key entry window

recommendations.thesis: WTI crude surged 7.3% to $73 Friday on fears of Strait of Hormuz disruption. ExxonMobil, producing 4.7M BOE/day, directly benefits from sustained elevated oil prices and geopolitical supply risk premium.
recommendations.catalyst: Israel-Iran conflict drives oil to 6-month highs; Strait of Hormuz supply disruption risk

catalystClarity.reasoning: Oil price mechanism is direct and clear: geopolitical disruption to Middle East supply routes boosts prices, which directly lifts integrated oil company earnings

technicalSetup.reasoning: XOM broke above recent resistance Friday on volume; prior YTD trend already positive

riskReward.reasoning: Target $163 (+5.2%), stop $150 (-3.2%), R/R ~1.6:1 — good asymmetry if oil stays elevated

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: XOM is one of the largest and most liquid US equities; institutional flows expected given oil move

marketAlignment.reasoning: Broader market weak Friday; energy sector was the bright spot — sector rotation into energy is partial tailwind

informationEdge.reasoning: 7.3% oil spike is well-documented pre-market; Brent at $74 suggests sustained premium baked in

timingUrgency.reasoning: IEA June report flagged supply disruption risks; oil market likely to maintain risk premium until ceasefire clarity

recommendations.thesis: United Airlines fell 4.4% Friday as oil surged 7.3% — jet fuel is the largest airline operating cost. With WTI near $73 and potential for further escalation, airline margins face sustained compression.
recommendations.catalyst: Oil prices at 6-month highs (+7.3% Friday); airline fuel cost sensitivity; possible further Iran retaliation

catalystClarity.reasoning: Oil surge is clearly documented; UAL's Q2 guidance did not anticipate $72-73 WTI; fuel cost pass-through has a lag

technicalSetup.reasoning: UAL broke below near-term support Friday; bearish momentum with airlines the worst sector Friday alongside cruise lines

riskReward.reasoning: Target $68 (-8.1%), stop $78 (+5.4%), R/R ~1.5:1 — reasonable asymmetry for a catalyst-driven short

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: UAL is highly liquid; short interest manageable; institutional short coverage available

marketAlignment.reasoning: Risk-off environment supports defensive positioning; airlines were specifically called out as Friday's biggest losers

informationEdge.reasoning: NBC/CBS reporting oil at multi-year high and airlines specifically hit; Norwegian Cruise down 5%, Delta -3.8% corroborates sector weakness

timingUrgency.reasoning: Iran's full retaliation response may not yet be complete; pre-market futures already reflecting continued tension

outcomes.notes: Opened lower as Iran signaled de-escalation willingness (WSJ report); defense stocks reversed sharply. Stop at $478 triggered early in session. Closed at $467, down ~4% from Friday's $491.95 close.

Oil fell ~2% Monday as Iran de-escalation reports reduced geopolitical risk premium. XOM drifted lower all session but did not formally hit the $150 stop. Broad energy sector underperformed on the day.

UAL gapped up at open on Iran de-escalation news; oil fell and airline stocks surged. UAL rose +6.11% closing at $78.12. Short position hit stop at $78 early in session. Worst possible outcome for short thesis.

recommendations.thesis: Trump sweep guarantees bank deregulation; JPM is the premier beneficiary as largest US bank with clearest upside from Dodd-Frank rollback, M&A boom, and lighter capital requirements.
recommendations.catalyst: Trump elected 47th President with Republican Senate sweep; bank deregulation the most immediate policy trade

catalystClarity.reasoning: Republican sweep confirmed overnight; bank deregulation is the single clearest policy trade with immediate repricing expected

technicalSetup.reasoning: Financials range-bound pre-election, setting up a breakout on confirmed catalyst; gap-open expected but still below technical resistance

riskReward.reasoning: Target $228 (+7.0%), stop $205 (-3.8%), R/R 1.85:1 — solid asymmetry on a binary catalyst

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: JPM is the most liquid large-cap bank; institutional flows guaranteed at open; tight spreads expected

marketAlignment.reasoning: S&P futures +2.2%, Dow futures +2.84%, Russell +3%; broad risk-on tailwind confirms sector move

informationEdge.reasoning: Trump sweep with Senate control confirmed by 3am ET; deregulation path is unambiguous and immediate

timingUrgency.reasoning: One-time election catalyst with maximum impact on day 1; entering at open captures the full repricing event

recommendations.thesis: Elon Musk was Trump's most visible campaign surrogate; DOGE advisory role and regulatory favoritism for autonomous driving make Tesla a direct Trump-trade proxy stock.
recommendations.catalyst: Musk's political investment paying off; expected DOGE role, regulatory relief for FSD, Cybertruck production benefits

catalystClarity.reasoning: Musk-Trump alliance is the most covered individual stock story of election night; direct and concrete expected benefits

technicalSetup.reasoning: TSLA in uptrend pre-election; gap-up expected but momentum is strongly bullish heading into catalyst

riskReward.reasoning: Target $285 (+12.6%), stop $240 (-5.1%), R/R 2.5:1 — excellent asymmetry given magnitude of expected move

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: Mega-cap, one of the highest-volume US stocks; institutional and retail interest both guaranteed

marketAlignment.reasoning: Nasdaq futures +3%; tech broadly higher; TSLA benefits from both risk-on and specific Musk catalyst

informationEdge.reasoning: Musk's campaign role and expected advisory positions widely reported overnight; clear causal link to stock appreciation

timingUrgency.reasoning: Musk is the most talked-about private citizen on election night; maximum media attention and retail FOMO expected at open

recommendations.thesis: Trump explicitly promised to fire SEC Chair Gensler, establish a strategic Bitcoin reserve, and make the US the crypto capital of the world — the most pro-crypto platform in presidential history.
recommendations.catalyst: Trump elected; Bitcoin hitting all-time highs above $75,000 pre-market; Gensler removal expected; regulatory regime change

catalystClarity.reasoning: Trump's crypto promises were the most specific of any candidate; Bitcoin ATH pre-market is the clearest real-time confirmation

technicalSetup.reasoning: COIN had been building a base; crypto stocks underperformed before election but setup is clean for breakout

riskReward.reasoning: Target $240 (+27.7%), stop $170 (-9.6%), R/R 2.9:1 — exceptional asymmetry if regulatory regime changes

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: COIN is highly liquid; crypto community retail attention will drive heavy volume

marketAlignment.reasoning: Bitcoin +8% to ATH pre-market; crypto sector broadly surging; Coinbase is the direct liquid proxy

informationEdge.reasoning: Trump's crypto platform was the most explicitly pro-crypto in history; specific promises are directly actionable

timingUrgency.reasoning: Bitcoin ATH acts as immediate catalyst signal; COIN historically tracks BTC moves with leverage on election-type catalysts

recommendations.thesis: Goldman Sachs is the purest play on M&A revival and investment banking deregulation; Republican sweep eliminates tighter capital rules and unlocks deal-making environment.
recommendations.catalyst: Trump election win; Republican Senate; M&A deregulation; lighter capital requirements; IPO market revival expected

catalystClarity.reasoning: Deregulation of M&A and capital markets is the most concrete Goldman Sachs catalyst; IB revenues directly tied to deal volume

technicalSetup.reasoning: GS already in strong uptrend YTD (+57%); election win adds fundamental re-rating on top of momentum

riskReward.reasoning: Target $540 (+7.4%), stop $484 (-3.8%), R/R ~2:1 — appropriate for large-cap with clear catalyst

volumeLiquidity.reasoning: Highly liquid; institutional investors will rotateinto Goldman as the IB deregulation proxy immediately

marketAlignment.reasoning: Same broad financials tailwind as JPM; sector-wide repricing as deregulation becomes the base case

informationEdge.reasoning: Republican sweep clears path for maximum deregulation scenario; Goldman specifically benefits from M&A and trading environment

timingUrgency.reasoning: Market will reprice the deregulation premium in a single day; entering at open is critical to capture the full move

outcomes.notes: JPM surged 11.5% on November 6. Target of $228 hit on open gap; closed well above target at $237.50. Stop at $205 never threatened. Financials were the best-performing S&P sector on the day (+5.1% sector). Multiple sources confirm JPM +11.5%.

Tesla closed at $288.53 on November 6, up 14.75% from Nov 5 close of $251.44 (14.03% from entry $253). Target of $285 hit intraday. Elon Musk's DOGE role confirmed as major catalyst. Stop at $240 never approached.

Coinbase surged 31% on November 6 as crypto stocks exploded post-Trump win. Bitcoin hit $75K+ ATH pre-market. COIN closed ~$243.66, clearing $240 target. Coinbase soared 65% for the month of November. Stop at $170 never in play.

Goldman Sachs rose approximately 8.5% on November 6 as financials broadly surged on deregulation expectations. Target of $540 cleared. GS closed ~$545.76. Stop at $484 never threatened. Goldman was specifically cited as one of the biggest financial sector winners.
